rationale: The pair is topically and conceptually close: the citing paper studies private-label imitation/trade dress effects and explicitly discusses brand confusion and litigation-oriented harm metrics, while the cited paper develops a confusion-detection method and metric for visual copycat packages. Given this strong overlap in constructs (imitation, visual similarity, confusion, legal/managerial metrics), the citation is plausibly methodological or foundational rather than an obvious incidental mention.
